subheadline: AI-powered project management that removes the work around work.
value_proposition: Jira uses AI (Rovo) to automate administrative overhead so teams can focus on high-impact work instead of busywork.
target_audience: Teams of all sizes across marketing, engineering, design, operations, and IT — particularly those managing projects with multiple tools and seeking AI-augmented workflows.

testimonials: [Jira] makes life easier. [We] can spend time on adding value, not busywork. — Joe Cotant, Senior Technical Program Manager
press: Gartner Magic Quadrant 2025 — Leader in Collaborative Work Management, Marketing, and DevOps

pricing.price_anchoring: Free tier emphasized via 'Get it free' CTA; pricing page linked but not surfaced on this page

features: Rovo AI for planning — break down ideas, polish descriptions, link resources
AI agents to create project plans, identify trends, and flag risks
Goals alignment — connect work to company-level objectives
Automation — update statuses, pull integration data, assign agent tasks via Studio
Teamwork Graph — surfaces context from every company resource for faster decision-making
differentiators: Only platform recognized as a Leader in Gartner MQ for Collaborative Work Management, Marketing, AND DevOps
Native AI agents (Rovo) deeply integrated — not bolted on
MCP Gallery for orchestrating entire tool stack from one workspace
Teamwork Collection bundles Jira, Confluence, Loom, and Rovo together
trust_signals: Gartner Magic Quadrant 2025 Leader recognition (three categories)
Customer story from named individual at enterprise org
Atlassian brand reputation implied via footer product suite

design_patterns.layout: Full-width hero with email capture, followed by use-case tabs, feature highlight sections, social proof, and a closing CTA — standard SaaS top-down narrative flow
design_patterns.color_scheme: Atlassian blue primary, white backgrounds, dark text; minimal use of accent colors
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif; large bold headlines, lighter body copy
design_patterns.visual_style: Minimal and professional with aspirational copy; product video in hero section
notable_patterns: Email capture in hero (not just a button)
Tabbed use-case selector (Marketing / Engineering / Design / Operations / IT)

emotional_tone: Aspirational and confident — positions AI as empowering rather than threatening, framing teams as 'human-AI super teams'
key_objections_addressed: Too much admin overhead — directly addressed in headline and subheadline
Works for different team types — tabbed use cases show breadth
Too expensive to try — 'Get it free' lowers entry barrier
missing_or_weak: No customer logos visible — significant trust gap for enterprise buyers
No quantified metrics (e.g., '10M teams', 'saves X hours/week') to anchor value
Only one testimonial, from an unnamed company — weak social proof density
Pricing not surfaced at all on the page — friction for self-serve evaluators
No comparison to competitors (Asana, Monday, Linear) to aid switching decisions
No FAQ or objection-handling section for common hesitations
Feature descriptions are abstract ('Surface related work') rather than concrete outcomes
No free trial countdown or urgency mechanism beyond the webinar banner
standout_elements: Gartner tri-category leadership claim is a strong, specific credibility anchor
Email capture in hero rather than generic CTA button increases intent signaling
Framing AI as 'human-AI super team' is differentiated and non-threatening positioning
MCP Gallery callout signals technical sophistication to developer/eng audiences
Teamwork Collection bundling creates upsell narrative without feeling pushy
